page: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU instance within the Cloud GPU family. It features 4 shared vCPUs mapped to an Intel Broadwell architecture running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 20.0 GB of system memory. The graphics acceleration is delivered via a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU allocation providing 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 360 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates highly polarized performance characteristics. The instance excels in LLM inference workloads, achieving top-tier results for both prompt processing and text generation using small and medium models. Conversely, CPU-bound cryptographic operations and database operations register as weak, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor. General CPU and memory benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Passmark, fall within the average range.

By utilizing a shared CPU allocation and a fractional GPU slice, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring GPU acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated card. However, the shared CPU architecture and lower cryptographic performance represent a clear tradeoff for compute-heavy or highly secure database applications. This server is best suited for lightweight machine learning inference, small-to-medium LLM execution, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from moderate GPU acceleration.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU instance featuring 4 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs at 2.0 GHz, 20.0 GB of RAM, and a 360 GB NVMe SSD. It includes a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU providing 8 GB of VRAM. Performance benchmarks show top-tier capabilities in LLM inference for small and medium models, alongside average general CPU and memory performance. However, tradeoffs include weak cryptographic and database performance, as well as poor multi-threaded decompression. This configuration offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring GPU acceleration without the overhead of a dedicated card. It is best suited for lightweight machine learning inference, small-scale model execution, and general-purpose tasks requiring moderate graphics processing.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU server featuring 4 shared Intel vCPUs, 20 GB RAM, a 360 GB NVMe SSD, and an 8 GB NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU slice, optimized for lightweight LLM inference.

tagline: A fractional GPU instance combining shared Intel processors with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for cost-efficient machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 8 GB VRAM
4 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 20.0 GB system memory
Top-tier performance in small and medium LLM inference
Weak performance in cryptographic and database operations
Suited for lightweight machine learning and GPU-assisted workloads
